name: Montealegre del Castillo and Alpera —
name_original: Montealegre del Castillo y Alpera — circular desde Almansa
description: This moderate 49.7-mile road cycling route from Almansa explores diverse landscapes and historical sites like Almansa Castle.
region_intro: Road cycling around Almansa offers a varied experience across plains and mountain systems in the province of Albacete, Spain. The terrain features a mix of open landscapes and higher elevations, influenced by a Mediterranean mountain climate and limestone-rich soil. Cyclists encounter Aleppo pine forests, black pine in shadier areas, and Mediterranean scrubland, providing diverse natural backdrops. The region's natural features include the Sierra de Almansa mountain range and the historic Almansa Reservoir. Best road cycling routes around Almansa The most popular road cycling route is Montealegre del Castillo and Alpera —, a 49.7 miles (80.0 km) trail that takes 3 hours 17 minutes to complete. This moderate route traverses varied landscapes between the towns of Montealegre del Castillo and Alpera. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Almansa Castle loop from Almansa, a moderate 38.8 miles (62.5 km) path. This route offers views of the iconic Almansa Castle and the surrounding plains. Local road cyclists also love the Almansa Castle – Alpera loop from Almansa, a 43.2 miles (69.5 km) trail leading through the region's characteristic plains and near the Almansa Castle, often completed in about 3 hours 40 minutes. Road cycling around Almansa is defined by plains, mountain ranges like Sierra de Almansa, and pine forests. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to different ability levels. The routes in Almansa are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from a few reviews. More than 1000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Almansa's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when road cycling around Almansa?
A: Almansa offers a diverse road cycling experience, featuring a mix of open plains and mountain systems like the Sierra de Almansa. The region's Mediterranean mountain climate and limestone-rich soil influence the landscape, which includes Aleppo pine forests, black pine in higher areas, and Mediterranean scrubland. You'll find routes with varying elevation gains, from flatter sections across plains to more challenging climbs in the mountains.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners or families in Almansa?
A: Yes, Almansa provides options for various skill levels. While many routes offer moderate to difficult challenges, there are also easier routes available. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38160447" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Palace of the Counts of Cirat – Castle of Almansa loop from Ruta de Levante&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate route that could be suitable for those looking for a less strenuous ride, offering a distance of approximately 47.7 km.

Q: What historical landmarks or natural features can I see along the road cycling routes?
A: Almansa's routes often incorporate significant historical and natural sights. You can cycle past the iconic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5287878" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Almansa&lt;/a&gt;, a 14th-century medieval fortress, or the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5674312" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of the Assumption&lt;/a&gt;. Natural features include the historic Almansa Reservoir and various dehesas with diverse vegetation. For panoramic views, consider stopping at the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4713383" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viewpoint of the Museum Trail&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there challenging road cycling routes for experienced cyclists?
A: Absolutely. Almansa offers several difficult routes for experienced cyclists seeking a challenge. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20131955" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Almansa – Palace of the Counts of Cirat loop from Almansa&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult route covering approximately 89 km with significant elevation gain, providing a demanding yet rewarding experience.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Almansa?
A: Given Almansa's Mediterranean mountain climate, spring and autumn are generally the most pleasant seasons for road cycling. Temperatures are milder, and the natural scenery is vibrant. Summers can be hot, especially in the open plains, while winters are cooler but often still suitable for cycling on sunny days.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes available around Almansa?
A: Yes, many of the road cycling routes around Almansa are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Examples include the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38160016" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almansa Castle loop from Almansa&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38160136" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almansa Castle – Alpera loop from Almansa&lt;/a&gt;, which offer scenic circular journeys.

Q: What do other road cyclists say about the routes in Almansa?
A: The road cycling routes in Almansa are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars from several reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, the well-paved surfaces, and the opportunity to combine physical activity with exploring historical sites and natural beauty.

Q: How many road cycling routes are available in the Almansa region?
A: There are over 15 road cycling routes available around Almansa, catering to a range of distances and difficulty levels. These routes allow you to explore the diverse landscapes of the region, from plains to mountain ranges.

Q: What are the typical distances and elevation gains for road cycling routes in Almansa?
A: Routes in Almansa vary significantly. You can find moderate routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1168128366/montealegre-del-castillo-and-alpera" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montealegre del Castillo and Alpera —&lt;/a&gt;, which covers nearly 80 km with about 450 meters of elevation gain. More challenging routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20131955" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Almansa – Palace of the Counts of Cirat loop from Almansa&lt;/a&gt;, can exceed 89 km with over 950 meters of climbing.

Q: Are the road surfaces generally good for road bikes in Almansa?
A: Yes, the region is well-suited for road cycling, with many routes featuring mostly well-paved surfaces. This makes them ideal for road bikes, allowing for smooth and enjoyable rides across the varied landscapes.

Q: Where can I find parking for road cycling trails in Almansa?
A: While specific parking locations for each trailhead are not detailed, routes often start and end in or near the town of Almansa. You can typically find public parking within the town or at designated points near the start of popular routes, especially those that loop from Almansa itself.
tips: Nice town worth a visit. If you head south, you will come to La Mejorada park, where there is a rest area with picnic tables and drinking fountains.

The castle of Almohad origin located on the hill of the Eagle. It dates from the 14th century. It is possible to visit it. Every year a historical re-enactment of the 1707 battle is held, known as the Battle of Almansa.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start the Montealegre del Castillo y Alpera circular route?
faqs.answer: The route starts and ends in Almansa. You can typically find parking in or around the town center, which serves as a convenient starting point for this circular road cycling tour. Specific parking areas are not detailed, but Almansa is well-equipped for visitors.

faqs.question: Is the Montealegre del Castillo y Alpera circular route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: This is a road cycling route, so while dogs are generally not suitable companions for a racebike tour of this distance and duration, there are no specific restrictions mentioned for the route itself. If you plan to visit towns like Alpera or Almansa Castle, local pet policies would apply.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The region generally offers pleasant cycling conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn are often ideal, providing milder temperatures than the peak of summer. Always check the local weather forecast before you head out.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this road cycling route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle the Montealegre del Castillo y Alpera circular route. It uses public roads and paths.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this road cycling route?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as a moderate road ride. The surfaces are generally well-paved and easy to ride, suitable for a racebike. However, be prepared for some segments that *may* be unpaved and more challenging, requiring good bike handling skills.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Given its 'moderate' difficulty rating, distance of nearly 80 km, and around 450 meters of elevation gain, this route is best suited for cyclists with a good level of fitness. Beginners might find it challenging, but experienced riders will enjoy a substantial outing.

faqs.question: What points of interest can I see along the Montealegre del Castillo y Alpera circular route?
faqs.answer: The route passes through or near interesting locations. You'll cycle through the settlement of Alpera and start/end near Almansa Castle. The broader region also features natural monuments like Monte Arabí, known for its unique geomorphology and ancient cave paintings, which may offer scenic views along the way.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or stay near the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, both Almansa and Alpera are settlements where you can find cafes, restaurants, and accommodation options. Almansa, as the starting and ending point, offers a wider range of services for cyclists.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this road cycling tour?
faqs.answer: For this moderate road cycling tour, we recommend bringing plenty of water, snacks, a repair kit for your bike, appropriate cycling attire, sun protection, and a fully charged phone. Given the duration, consider bringing enough provisions to last the entire ride or plan stops in towns.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this road cycling route?
faqs.answer: On average, completing this 80 km route takes approximately 3 hours and 17 minutes of active cycling time. This duration can vary based on your fitness level, speed, and any stops you make along the way.

faqs.question: Is this a loop trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Montealegre del Castillo y Alpera route is a circular road cycling tour, meaning it starts and ends in the same location, Almansa.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route briefly intersects with sections of the Camino de Santiago de Levante, Camino del Alba, Camino de San José, Camino de Carrabera, Camino de la Pedriza, Paseo de las Encinas, and the Vereda Real Andalucía a Valencia.
